reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the swap cache being tightly coupled to physical swap infrastructure by announcing plans to decouple it and move it to the virtual swap layer, where it will be managed at the virtual swap layer.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the organization and categorization of the swap API in include/linux/swap.h, promising to restructure it as part of a clean-up effort without any functional changes intended.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2, and provided a generic API abstraction for this behavior.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary:
The author addressed a concern about the zswap entry operations being tightly coupled to the physical swap layer by adding new helper functions to abstract away these operations, facilitating re-implementation when swap is virtualized.

Author addressed a concern about the swap_zswap_tree() function, which was previously using xa_store(), xa_load(), and xa_erase() macros to manipulate the xarray tree. The author replaced these macros with new inline functions zswap_entry_store(), zswap_entry_load(), and zswap_entry_erase() that directly call the corresponding xarray operations.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the swapcached state being managed at the virtual swap layer by abstracting away a function in userfaultfd.c that directly looks at the swapfile's swap map to check if a swap entry is cached.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the swap virtualization implementation, specifically the addition of new code for the vswap debugfs directory without any behavioral changes intended. The author has added scaffolds for the new code and set up vswap compilation in the Makefile, with no changes to existing behavior.
reviews.raw_body:
In prepration for the implementation of swap virtualization, add new
scaffolds for the new code: a new mm/vswap.c source file, which
currently only holds the logic to set up the (for now, empty) vswap
debugfs directory. Hook this up in the swap setup step in
mm/swap_state.c, and set up vswap compilation in the Makefile.

Other than the debugfs directory, no behavioral change intended.

Finally, make Johannes a swap reviewer, given that he has contributed
majorly to the developments of virtual swap.

Signed-off-by: Nhat Pham <nphamcs@gmail.com>
---
 MAINTAINERS          |  2 ++
 include/linux/swap.h |  3 +++
 mm/Makefile          |  2 +-
 mm/swap_state.c      |  6 ++++++
 mm/vswap.c           | 35 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 5 files changed, 47 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
 create mode 100644 mm/vswap.c

diff --git a/MAINTAINERS b/MAINTAINERS
index e087673237636..b21038b160a07 100644
--- a/MAINTAINERS
+++ b/MAINTAINERS
@@ -16728,6 +16728,7 @@ R:	Kemeng Shi <shikemeng@huaweicloud.com>
 R:	Nhat Pham <nphamcs@gmail.com>
 R:	Baoquan He <bhe@redhat.com>
 R:	Barry Song <baohua@kernel.org>
+R:	Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>
 L:	linux-mm@kvack.org
 S:	Maintained
 F:	Documentation/mm/swap-table.rst
@@ -16740,6 +16741,7 @@ F:	mm/swap.h
 F:	mm/swap_table.h
 F:	mm/swap_state.c
 F:	mm/swapfile.c
+F:	mm/vswap.c
 
 MEMORY MANAGEMENT - THP (TRANSPARENT HUGE PAGE)
 M:	Andrew Morton <akpm@linux-foundation.org>
diff --git a/include/linux/swap.h b/include/linux/swap.h
index 918b47da55f44..df0771903a952 100644
--- a/include/linux/swap.h
+++ b/include/linux/swap.h
@@ -423,6 +423,9 @@ extern void __meminit kswapd_stop(int nid);
 
 #ifdef CONFIG_SWAP
 
+/* Virtual swap space API (mm/vswap.c) */
+int vswap_init(void);
+
 /* Lifecycle swap API (mm/swapfile.c) */
 int folio_alloc_swap(struct folio *folio);
 bool folio_free_swap(struct folio *folio);
diff --git a/mm/Makefile b/mm/Makefile
index 2d0570a16e5be..67fa4586e7e18 100644
--- a/mm/Makefile
+++ b/mm/Makefile
@@ -75,7 +75,7 @@ ifdef CONFIG_MMU
 	obj-$(CONFIG_ADVISE_SYSCALLS)	+= madvise.o
 endif
 
-obj-$(CONFIG_SWAP)	+= page_io.o swap_state.o swapfile.o
+obj-$(CONFIG_SWAP)	+= page_io.o swap_state.o swapfile.o vswap.o
 obj-$(CONFIG_ZSWAP)	+= zswap.o
 obj-$(CONFIG_HAS_DMA)	+= dmapool.o
 obj-$(CONFIG_HUGETLBFS)	+= hugetlb.o hugetlb_sysfs.o hugetlb_sysctl.o
diff --git a/mm/swap_state.c b/mm/swap_state.c
index e2e9f55bea3bb..29ec666be4204 100644
--- a/mm/swap_state.c
+++ b/mm/swap_state.c
@@ -882,6 +882,12 @@ static int __init swap_init(void)
 	int err;
 	struct kobject *swap_kobj;
 
+	err = vswap_init();
+	if (err) {
+		pr_err("failed to initialize virtual swap space\n");
+		return err;
+	}
+
 	swap_kobj = kobject_create_and_add("swap", mm_kobj);
 	if (!swap_kobj) {
 		pr_err("failed to create swap kobject\n");
diff --git a/mm/vswap.c b/mm/vswap.c
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000..e68234f053fc9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/mm/vswap.c
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+// SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0
+/*
+ * Virtual swap space
+ *
+ * Copyright (C) 2024 Meta Platforms, Inc., Nhat Pham
+ */
+#include <linux/swap.h>
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_DEBUG_FS
+#include <linux/debugfs.h>
+
+static struct dentry *vswap_debugfs_root;
+
+static int vswap_debug_fs_init(void)
+{
+	if (!debugfs_initialized())
+		return -ENODEV;
+
+	vswap_debugfs_root = debugfs_create_dir("vswap", NULL);
+	return 0;
+}
+#else
+static int vswap_debug_fs_init(void)
+{
+	return 0;
+}
+#endif
+
+int vswap_init(void)
+{
+	if (vswap_debug_fs_init())
+		pr_warn("Failed to initialize vswap debugfs\n");
+
+	return 0;
+}
-- 
2.52.0

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the separation of logical and physical swap slot representations by introducing new types (swp_slot_t) to represent physical swap slots, which will be used in later patches for virtual swap allocation. The author noted that no behavioral changes are made yet, but this is a preparatory step for future patches.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledged that the zswap tree code can no longer be reused for the new virtual swap space design and decided to use a simple unified zswap tree in the new implementation.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the freeing ordering of virtual swap slots, agreeing to restructure in v2 to clear the shadow, invalidate the zswap entry, and uncharge swap cgroup before releasing the virtual swap slot.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: The author apologized for accidentally copying their draft cover letter over the diffstat, and provided a link to the correct base commit (05f7e89ab9731565d8a62e3b5d1ec206485eeb0b) which is on top of 6.19.
reviews.raw_body:
[...]

Argh I copied my draft cover letter over the diffstat. My apologies -
here's the rest of the cover letter. Base commit is
05f7e89ab9731565d8a62e3b5d1ec206485eeb0b for your convenience (it's at
the bottom too - this should be 6.19).

Nhat Pham (21):
  mm/swap: decouple swap cache from physical swap infrastructure
  swap: rearrange the swap header file
  mm: swap: add an abstract API for locking out swapoff
  zswap: add new helpers for zswap entry operations
  mm/swap: add a new function to check if a swap entry is in swap
    cached.
  mm: swap: add a separate type for physical swap slots
  mm: create scaffolds for the new virtual swap implementation
  zswap: prepare zswap for swap virtualization
  mm: swap: allocate a virtual swap slot for each swapped out page
  swap: move swap cache to virtual swap descriptor
  zswap: move zswap entry management to the virtual swap descriptor
  swap: implement the swap_cgroup API using virtual swap
  swap: manage swap entry lifecycle at the virtual swap layer
  mm: swap: decouple virtual swap slot from backing store
  zswap: do not start zswap shrinker if there is no physical swap slots
  swap: do not unnecesarily pin readahead swap entries
  swapfile: remove zeromap bitmap
  memcg: swap: only charge physical swap slots
  swap: simplify swapoff using virtual swap
  swapfile: replace the swap map with bitmaps
  vswap: batch contiguous vswap free calls

 include/linux/cpuhotplug.h      |    1 +
 include/linux/memcontrol.h      |    6 +

 include/linux/zswap.h           |   17 +-

 mm/huge_memory.c                |   11 +-

 mm/madvise.c                    |    2 +-
 mm/memcontrol-v1.c              |    6 +
 mm/memcontrol.c                 |  144 ++-
 mm/memory.c                     |  109 +-
 mm/migrate.c                    |   13 +-
 mm/mincore.c                    |   15 +-
 mm/page_io.c                    |   83 +-
 mm/shmem.c                      |  227 +---
 mm/swap.h                       |  179 +--
 mm/swap_cgroup.c                |  172 ---
 mm/swap_state.c                 |  306 +----
 mm/swap_table.h                 |   78 +-
 mm/swapfile.c                   | 1517 ++++-------------------
 mm/userfaultfd.c                |   18 +-
 mm/vmscan.c                     |   28 +-
 mm/vswap.c                      | 2008 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 mm/zswap.c                      |  142 +--
 32 files changed, 2942 insertions(+), 2507 deletions(-)

 create mode 100644 mm/vswap.c


base-commit: 05f7e89ab9731565d8a62e3b5d1ec206485eeb0b

2.52.0

reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the guard(rcu)() call is missing, which can lead to a deadlock when vswap_free() acquires the per-vswap spinlock while holding the folio lock

The reviewer noted that the patch fails to compile under PREEMPT_RT due to the use of a spinlock, and requested it be replaced with a raw_spinlock_t.

Reviewer Peter Zijlstra requested the use of lockdep_assert_held() to ensure that cluster->lock is held before accessing it, citing a potential lock ordering issue.

reviewer noted that the code path being executed in this patch is not suitable for a DEAD callback with IRQs disabled, creating potential issues

reviewer noted that the patch's use of xas_alloc() in a DEAD callback is not allowed and could lead to issues
reviews.raw_body:
nit:
	guard(rcu)();

---

This breaks on PREEMPT_RT as this is ran with IRQs disabled. This must
be a raw_spinlock_t.

---

This is terrible, please use:

	lockdep_assert_held(&cluster->lock);

---

This is again broken, this cannot be from a DEAD callback with IRQs
disabled.

---

Strictly speaking this can end up in xas_alloc(), which is again, not
allowed in a DEAD callback.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the need to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap() on the swapoff path, agreed to restructure in a future version (v2) and suggested deferring free operations using call_rcu() or workqueue.
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm seems like we can just defer-free on the cpu_dead path, and that
should be safe?

Right now, if a cluster is cached on a CPU, we know that it's not
cached on any other CPU, and it's not on any other partial lists.
Maybe can call_rcu() here to defer-free it. Hopefully cpu dead is rare
enough of an event that we dont have a backlog of free deferrals :)

The other alternative is workqueue (with some careful rcu handling in
the free callback), but that seems unnecessary.

reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary: Reviewer Peter Zijlstra clarified a misunderstanding regarding swap states, noting that 'DEAD' swap entries are acceptable but 'DYING' is the problematic state.
reviews.raw_body:
Oh, I think I might have confused DEAD and DYING here. DYING is the
tricky one, DEAD should be okay. Sorry about that.

reviews.author: syzbot ci
reviews.summary: The reviewer, syzbot ci, detected a possible deadlock in the vswap_iter function due to the acquisition of the per-vswap spinlock while holding the folio lock, which creates a lock ordering violation with reclaim paths.
reviews.raw_body:

* [PATCH v4 13/21] swap: manage swap entry lifecycle at the virtual swap layer
* [PATCH v4 14/21] mm: swap: decouple virtual swap slot from backing store
* [PATCH v4 15/21] zswap: do not start zswap shrinker if there is no physical swap slots
* [PATCH v4 16/21] swap: do not unnecesarily pin readahead swap entries

and found the following issue:
possible deadlock in vswap_iter

Full report is available here:
https://ci.syzbot.org/series/f8238a2a-370e-404d-b3f7-5945b574bd63

***

possible deadlock in vswap_iter

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch series implements virtual swap space, a new approach to handling swap operations in Linux. The current method ties a swap entry to its backing slot, which can lead to performance issues when swap is used as a cache or optimized. This series introduces a cluster-based allocation algorithm for virtual swap slots, reducing lock contention and memory overhead. It also reuses the swap table for reverse mapping, removes unnecessary components like the swap cache and zswap tree, and reduces the size of the swap descriptor.
